data.title: 'We Are Entering The Age Of The One-Person Unicorn,' Alibaba.com President Says
data.description:
Solopreneurs will soon be able to launch billion-dollar companies all by themselves with the help of AI, Alibaba.com President Kuo Zhang says.

"The ‘Execution Wall' that once separated the solo entrepreneur from the multinational corporation is crumbling – not because the giants are fading, but because the tools of scale have finally been democratized," Zhang said in an opinion piece for Fortune published Monday. "We are entering the age of the One-Person Unicorn."

Don't Miss:

Zhang said AI agents will help Solopreneurs automate everything from procurement to compliance, compressing weeks-long processes to a few minutes.

The differentiating factor for businesses in this new paradigm will be imagination, judgement, taste and strategic vision, he said. While this potential future raises questions about employment, it also offers many opportunities to rise through the ranks, he added.

Trending: Skip the Regrets: The Essential Retirement Tips Experts Wish Everyone Knew Earlier.

Still, just how much AI currently benefits businesses remains in doubt.

For individuals looking to participate in the rise of tech-driven businesses, platforms like Public make it easy to invest in innovative companies and ETFs, giving solopreneurs and investors alike a way to capitalize on AI-driven growth.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Trump Disapproval Rating 54%: Voters Say Economy Should Be Top Priority But It's Not (CORRECTED)
data.description:
Editor's Note: The headline has been updated to correctly reflect the disapproval rating.

A new approval poll for President Donald Trump shows declining ratings since the U.S. attacked Iran, with voters more worried about the economy than attacks on other countries.

Trump's Approval Rating

In a new voter poll, Trump's disapproval rating holds steady at 54% with an approval rating of 43%.

The poll from Morning Consult shows minimal overall movement for Trump's ratings from the previous week, with the approval rating falling in previous weeks.

For comparison, Trump began his second presidential term with a 52% approval rating, matching his record high from March 2017 based on Morning Consult weekly polls.

At a similar time into Trump's first term, his approval rating was 42% with a disapproval rating of 51%, showing a similar trend from voters during the president's first term.

The new poll found that voters favor Democrats on a general ballot for the 2026 election at 45% to 42% for the Republican Party.

Economy Key Focus For Voters, But Not For President

The poll found that voters said lowering costs should be the top priority for the president at 72%. Only 47% of voters said they believe this is a top priority for the president.

Likewise, voters picked health care affordability and reducing energy prices as the second and third most important key priorities at 70% and 64% respectively. Only 45% and 42% of voters respectively said health care affordability and reducing energy prices were top priorities of President Trump.

On the overall economy, Trump gets a 42% approval rating with a 51% disapproval rating. The -9 net approval rating is near a record low of -10 for Trump's second presidential term set back in November 2025.

While Trump used to be able to brag about record highs for the stock market while his approval ratings tanked, the same cannot be said in recent weeks.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: If You Invested $1000 In Fortinet Stock 5 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:
Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 6.67% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 16.61%. Currently, Fortinet has a market capitalization of $58.01 billion.

Buying $1000 In FTNT: If an investor had bought $1000 of FTNT stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $2,128.82 today based on a price of $78.01 for FTNT at the time of writing.
